regression_log.reason: Stage 3 critique returned REWORK with 4 critical findings, the most severe being CF-1 (hook PPID = sh, not claude main — invalidates load-bearing empirical claim). CF-3 (WU1 missing process-tree traversal helper) and CF-4 (4 post-migration consumer breakage sites) require WU1 and WU5 to be reshaped. CF-5 (source='clear' unhandled), CF-6 (Q2 unverified empirically), CF-7 (uuidgen contradiction), CF-9 (rollback silently fails) require targeted spec patches. Empirical re-validation needed: SessionStart probe extension (full process tree), SessionEnd stdin probe, compaction probe.

describe.confidence_note: Brief + 2 vault findings + source survey gave a high-confidence triage. 7 steps cleanly decomposed. Open questions are correctly deferred to Stage 2 (empirical gates, not describe-stage items).

specify.rev1_confidence_note: [REGRESSED rev1→rev2] Comprehensive but load-bearing empirical claim was wrong. See spec.md.revision-1.bak.
specify.rev2_confidence_note: Rev2 addresses all 4 critical findings + 5 high + medium/low cleanups. Process-tree traversal helper (epistemic_claude_main_pid) replaces wrong PPID assumption. WU5 split into 5a-5d for per-file consumer fixes (CF-4). uuidgen fallback removed (CF-7). Rollback Plan made non-misleading (CF-9). AC4 rewritten with valid grep (CF-11). Probe v2 archived. 15 WUs. Confidence higher than rev1 because empirical foundation is now grounded; held below 0.95 because OQ-rev2-2 (eval simulation fidelity) is still a Stage 7 risk.
addressed_findings: CF-1, CF-2, CF-3 (PPID cascade) — process-tree traversal in helper
CF-4 (consumer breakage) — WU5 split 5a/b/c/d with per-file fix types
CF-5 (source=clear) — defensive default branch in WU2
CF-6 (Q2) — confirmed by probe v2; WU3 stdin fallback validated
CF-10 (FM4) — single helper mechanism, no per-file inline fallbacks
CF-11/15/18 (AC quality) — AC4 rewritten, test.sh extended, AC9/WU9 corrected
CF-16 (TESTED/VERIFIED) — vocabulary applied per row in AC table
CF-20 (sweep placement traceability) — recorded in failure modes table

challenge.rev2_findings_resolution: 19/20 FIXED, 1 PARTIAL (CF-20 trivial — recorded as OQ-rev2-3)
challenge.rev2_new_findings: 4 (NEW-1 medium resume wording, NEW-2 medium preflight re-submission constraint, NEW-3 low /proc unavailable warning, NEW-4 low manifest_stale)
challenge.rev2_new_findings_resolution: NEW-1 + NEW-2 + NEW-3 patched inline (Failure Modes + OQ-rev2-3); NEW-4 fixed by manifest.json regeneration on 2026-05-04T10:15Z
challenge.confidence_note: rev2 vanilla critique returned READY with strong finding-resolution audit. Inline patches applied for 4 minor new findings — none warranted regression. Critique pipeline produced clean signal both directions: REWORK on rev1 (well-grounded), READY on rev2 (well-grounded). System validated.

high_priority_cases: E1 (stat vs status parsing — traversal returns wrong PPid)
E2 (cross-filesystem atomic write — EXDEV on mv)
edge_cases.confidence_note: 12 boundaries enumerated, 2 genuine spec gaps found (E1/E2), 4 medium patches for hygiene/correctness (E3/E5/E6/E8), 6 low/watch items. No architectural rework required. Patches are inline WU1/WU2 constraints.

premortem.confidence_note: Pre-mortem produced 4 actionable operational findings. Top concern (PM-1 silent corrupt install) absorbed inline via PM-Fix-1 + PM-Fix-4 (post-install verification). Overlap with prior stages 50% — confirming pre-mortem is finding new operational angles, not repeating Stage 3/4 design/boundary findings. No regression.

review.skip_reason: Stage 3 Standard-tier critique (7 agents, 3 lenses) + Stage 4 vanilla edge-cases + Stage 4.5 pre-mortem produced ~50 findings already absorbed. Diminishing returns on additional external review for a shell hook refactor that doesn't match security/performance/frontend reviewer profiles. /gpt-review remains available if desired before Stage 7 execution.

test.confidence_note: Spec-blind tests derived only from AC table, Preservation Contract, Failure Modes, and Edge Case Constraints sections. Anti-tautology review applied; T-AC4 grep tests are intentionally adoption-checking (architectural invariant per AC4). Tests live as behavioral specs; eval fixtures (WU8-11) and test.sh extensions (WU12) provide automated execution. Manual checks (T-AC3 authoritative + T-AC10 timing) deferred to debrief verification.

execute.wu_consolidation_note: WU8-11 specified evals.json fixtures, but evals.json is fixture-regression format (regex on captured text), not behavioral simulation. Implemented as Phase 9-11 of scripts/epistemic-smoke-test.sh which performs actual subshell execution with EPISTEMIC_CLAUDE_MAIN_PID_OVERRIDE for parallel-isolation, resume-pairing, and migration. Wired into test.sh as Category 8.5. Behavioral coverage equivalent or stronger than fixture-based approach.

test_results.test_sh: 112 passed, 0 failed, 3 warnings (all pre-existing — shellcheck unavailable + unrelated clarify.md content)

ac_status.AC3: TESTED (proxy) — Phase 10; manual run authoritative (deferred to live /end)
ac_status.AC4: TESTED — test.sh AC4 grep checks: 17 helper consumers (>= 9), no direct reads outside helper+test
ac_status.AC9: TESTED — test.sh Category 5 extended for new helper
ac_status.AC10: VERIFIED (manual, deferred) — measure SessionStart timing on live session
ac_status.AC11: TESTED — Phase 6 fail-open paths including empty-stdin

deviations_from_spec: WU8-11 evals.json fixtures were folded into smoke-test phases instead of fixtures — see wu_consolidation_note. Spec assumed evals.json could host behavioral assertions; runtime found it is regex-on-text only.

Added EPISTEMIC_SKIP_SWEEP=1 env var to helper for test-only sweep bypass. Production behavior unchanged (var defaults off). This was needed because parallel-isolation tests use fake PIDs that the production sweep would correctly remove.
Phase 9-11 of smoke test use real bash subshells with override PIDs — same effect as the originally specified eval fixtures, slightly different host.
discoveries: /proc/$PID/status PPid line is tab-delimited (key:\tvalue). awk -F'\t' is robust; whitespace-split would break on comm names with spaces.

Hook ran during session: simulating preflight against live PID 2889563 clobbered the live session's marker. Required manual restoration. Future smoke tests should use EPISTEMIC_CLAUDE_MAIN_PID_OVERRIDE — added to debrief items.

Anti-pattern hooks fired twice during implementation: bash-rm-rf-with-variable on cleanup() preserved from original file, and bash-missing-fail-fast on test assertions. Both were addressed (guarded rm-rf, added -z guards). bash-missing-fail-fast continued to flag because the regex matches the shape regardless of guard presence — catalog notes this is expected (false positives intentional).

Manifest's current_stage field was 38 minutes stale relative to state.json — manifest_stale=false didn't catch it. Suggests manifest regeneration should run on every state.json stage transition, not just on regression.

debrief.ship_commit_note: Implementation in working tree; commit pending user approval per CLAUDE.md (do not commit unless asked).
debrief.live_verification: This session's /end invocation IS the end-to-end test. Marker at ~/.claude/.current-session/2889563 contains session_id 593d965a-9883-4c3b-8027-f2a7287e91cf. New hooks installed via bash install.sh.
discoveries: /proc/$PID/status is canonical PPid source — robust to comm names with spaces
Test-time clobber: simulating preflight against live PID overwrites your own marker — must use EPISTEMIC_CLAUDE_MAIN_PID_OVERRIDE
Sweep is correct; test fidelity bends via EPISTEMIC_SKIP_SWEEP=1, not by weakening sweep
Anti-pattern hooks fire on shape, not intent — guards added but hooks keep flagging (expected per catalog)
Manifest staleness not caught by manifest_stale=false — flag should flip on every stage transition, not just regression
deferred_items: OQ-rev2-1 subagent compatibility — out of scope, untested
AC10 timing budget — manual verification on live session deferred
PM-Fix-2 epistemic.json health probe — separate blueprint
wrong_assumptions: Rev1 PPID claim was wrong — fixed in rev2 by process-tree traversal
evals/evals.json could host behavioral tests — it's regex-on-text only; rerouted to smoke test
debrief.confidence_note: Implementation passes 112 test.sh checks + 29 smoke-test phases. Live verification (this session's /end) is the final acceptance gate. Held below 0.95 because (1) AC10 timing not yet measured and (2) AC3 manual resume verification deferred.

ambiguity_gate.rationale: Goal: 5 — 'replace single-file marker with per-process markers' is unambiguous; describe.md and brief converge. Constraint: 5 — in/out scope explicit including 'no compat shim' and 'no schema changes.' Success: 4 — behavioral eval is testable but Q1 (env-var existence) gates one criterion until Stage 2 resolves it.

prior_art_gate.reason: Prior art for this blueprint is 'what does Claude Code itself expose at hook fire time' — that's step 1 of the work breakdown and lives in Stage 2 (Q1 env-var investigation), not as a separate /prior-art run. No external library question to evaluate.

context.third_incident: 2026-05-01T17:08:43Z — DURING planning of this fix; marker clobbered by parallel claude PID 2533857; defensive snapshot at .claude/plans/epistemic-session-race/preflight-snapshot-original-2026-05-01T17-11-37Z.json; recovery instructions at RECOVERY-INSTRUCTIONS.md

empirical_findings.Q1_PPID_is_claude_main: RETRACTED 2026-05-04 — Original measurement was Bash-tool context only. Probe v2 (probe-v2-process-tree-and-sessionend.log) confirmed two distinct hierarchies: hook = bash → sh → claude (claude is PPID's PPID); Bash tool turn = bash → claude (claude is direct PPID). Resolution: process-tree traversal in shared helper finds claude main PID in both contexts (max 2 hops).
empirical_findings.Q1_resume_session_id: CONFIRMED 2026-05-04 — claude --resume preserves session_id but spawns new claude process with new PID. Marker contents (SESSION_ID from stdin) preserve identity across resume; marker filename (per-claude-PID) is just discovery scaffolding. Original preflight + resumed postflight pair correctly in epistemic.json under same session_id.
empirical_findings.Q2_sessionend_stdin: CONFIRMED 2026-05-04 — SessionEnd hook receives stdin JSON: {session_id, transcript_path, cwd, hook_event_name, reason}. No 'source' field (SessionEnd uses 'reason' instead). Reason values observed: 'other'. WU3 stdin fallback for session_id is reliable.
empirical_findings.Q3_compaction_unverified: DEFERRED — /compact source-value probe not run. CF-5 mitigation: defensive default branch in WU2 (any unrecognized source treated as startup with stderr warning).
empirical_findings.Q1_stdin_session_id_present: CONFIRMED via SessionStart probe (2026-05-01T17:30:55Z, archived at probe-sessionstart-stdin.log). Stdin JSON contains: session_id (UUIDv4), transcript_path, cwd, hook_event_name, source ('startup'|'resume'|'clear'), model. Claude Code's own session_id is reusable as our key — no need for uuidgen.
empirical_findings.Q1_hook_env_has_extras: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR and CLAUDE_ENV_FILE env vars are set FOR HOOKS but NOT for Claude turn Bash tool invocations. Confirmed by both probes. Hooks have richer env than turns.
empirical_findings.marker_anchor_decision_rev2: DECIDED 2026-05-04 — Option (A): per-claude-PID marker as discovery key, with claude main PID resolved via process-tree traversal (walk /proc/$PID/stat PPid chain until comm=claude). SESSION_ID from stdin JSON stored INSIDE marker as the canonical data identity. Rationale: (1) probe v2 confirms process tree is consistent (hook = bash→sh→claude, max 2 hops); (2) resume case works correctly because session_id is the data, marker filename is scaffolding; (3) smaller delta from existing session-end-cleanup.sh:18 convention; (4) no CLAUDE_ENV_FILE coupling (probe showed that var is per-hook-fire, not per-session). Option (B) session_id-keyed marker + by-PID index was considered and rejected — more moving parts for no correctness gain.
empirical_findings.claude_print_cleanup_works: Probe SessionStart fired correctly via `claude --print 'ping'`. SessionEnd cleanup deleted marker cleanly (single-session case). Demonstrates current cleanup logic works when scope is correct — fix is about scoping, not adding cleanup.
